docs/agent-context/learnings.md:32:- **Next.js app network boundary:** route protection and Neon auth middleware run through `[src/proxy.ts](../../src/proxy.ts)` with `PROTECTED_PREFIXES` (not a root `middleware.ts` in this repo). For **true anonymous** manual local `pnpm dev` sessions, shell `env -u DEV_AUTH_USER_ID` (etc.) does not override values loaded from `.env.local`; unset or comment dev-auth-related vars there and restart so anonymous browser sessions are trustworthy. `**LOCAL_PRODUCT_TESTING=false` alone is not enough:** in development, `DEV_AUTH_USER_ID` still makes server code treat requests as that user; clearing true anonymous behavior requires `**DEV_AUTH_USER_ID` unset** (not only toggling `LOCAL_PRODUCT_TESTING`). When running two local `next dev` servers in parallel on different ports, give each one a separate `distDir` (current smoke dirs: `.test-dist/next-smoke-anon` and `.test-dist/next-smoke-auth`) or they will fight over the build lock. `pnpm test:smoke` already handles env layering and dist dirs via `tests/helpers/smoke/mode-config.ts`. For multi-port Playwright smoke, `next.config.ts` `allowedDevOrigins` should list hostnames like `'127.0.0.1'` and `'localhost'`, not full URLs, so `/_next/*` assets are allowed during local cross-origin dev requests.
